docs.title: WSDP Documentation
docs.text:
Wi-Fi Sensing Data Processing

🌐 Official Platform: SDP8.org | 📦 PyPI: wsdp | 💻 GitHub: Source Code

A Python library for downloading, processing, analyzing and training on Wi-Fi CSI (Channel State Information) data.

Published and maintained by SDP8.org - the official platform for reproducible wireless sensing research.

docs.title: 🚀 Features
docs.text:
• Multi-dataset support: Widar, Gait, XRF55, ElderAL, ZTE datasets
  • Intelligent preprocessing: Wavelet denoising, phase calibration, signal resizing
  • Deep learning pipeline: End-to-end training with CNN + Transformer architecture
  • Authentication: JWT Token, email/password, or non-interactive mode
  • Visualization: Heatmaps, denoising comparison, phase calibration plots
  • Inference API: Simple predict() interface for deployment
  • CLI interface: Full command-line support for batch operations

docs.title: 📦 Installation
docs.text:
pip install wsdp

Or install from source:

git clone https://github.com/sdp-team/wsdp.git
cd wsdp
pip install -e .

docs.title: ⚡ Quick Start
docs.text: ⚠️ Prerequisite: Create a free account at SDP8.org — required for dataset downloads.

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from wsdp import pipeline, download

# Download dataset (SDP8.org account required)
download('widar', '/data/widar', email='you@example.com', password='yourpassword')

# Run pipeline
pipeline(
    input_path='/data/widar',
    output_folder='/output',
    dataset='widar',
)

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
# Download with SDP8.org credentials
wsdp download widar /data --email you@example.com --password yourpassword

# Or with JWT token (from SDP8.org dashboard)
wsdp download widar /data --token YOUR_JWT_TOKEN

# Run training
wsdp run /data /output widar --lr 0.001 --epochs 50

# List datasets
wsdp list --verbose

docs.title: WSDP API Reference

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Core Pipeline
  • Data Structures

  • Inference

docs.title: wsdp.pipeline()
docs.text:
Main training pipeline function.

from wsdp import pipeline

pipeline(
    input_path: str,
    output_folder: str,
    dataset: str,
    model_path: Optional[str] = None,
    learning_rate: Optional[float] = None,
    num_epochs: Optional[int] = None,
    batch_size: Optional[int] = None,
    config_file: Optional[str] = None,
)

Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description input_path str required Path to input data directory output_folder str required Path to output directory dataset str required Dataset name (widar, gait, xrf55, elderal, zte) model_path str None Path to custom model file learning_rate float None Learning rate (overrides config) num_epochs int None Number of epochs (overrides config) batch_size int None Batch size (overrides config) config_file str None Path to YAML config file

Example:

from wsdp import pipeline

pipeline(
    input_path='/data/widar',
    output_folder='/output',
    dataset='widar',
    learning_rate=1e-3,
    num_epochs=50,
)

docs.title: CSIData
docs.text:
Container for raw CSI data from a file.

from wsdp.structure import CSIData

data = CSIData(file_path="/path/to/file.dat")

Attributes: - file_path: str - Path to source file - raw_data: np.ndarray - Raw CSI data - metadata: dict - File metadata

docs.title: CSIFrame
docs.text:
Standardized frame structure for processed CSI.

from wsdp.structure import CSIFrame

frame = CSIFrame(
    csi_matrix: np.ndarray,  # Shape: (T, F, A) - Time, Frequency, Antenna
    timestamp: float,
    metadata: dict,
)

Attributes: - csi_matrix: np.ndarray - Complex CSI matrix (T, F, A) - timestamp: float - Frame timestamp - metadata: dict - Frame metadata

docs.title: Base Reader
docs.text:
from wsdp.readers import BaseReader

class MyReader(BaseReader):
    def read_file(self, file_path: str) -> CSIData:
        # Implementation
        pass

docs.title: Available Readers
docs.text:
Reader Dataset Format WidarReader Widar .dat (bfee) GaitReader Gait .dat (bfee) XRF55Reader XRF55 .npy ElderALReader ElderAL .csv ZTEReader ZTE .csv

Usage:

from wsdp.readers import WidarReader

reader = WidarReader()
data = reader.read_file("/path/to/widar.dat")

docs.title: Wavelet Denoising
docs.text:
from wsdp.algorithms import wavelet_denoise_csi

denoised = wavelet_denoise_csi(
    csi_data: np.ndarray,
    wavelet: str = 'db4',
    level: int = 2,
)

Parameters: - csi_data: np.ndarray - Input CSI data - wavelet: str - Wavelet type (default: 'db4') - level: int - Decomposition level (default: 2)

Returns: np.ndarray - Denoised CSI data

docs.title: Phase Calibration
docs.text:
from wsdp.algorithms import phase_calibration

calibrated = phase_calibration(
    csi_data: np.ndarray,
    method: str = 'linear',
)

Parameters: - csi_data: np.ndarray - Input CSI data - method: str - Calibration method ('linear' or 'quadratic')

Returns: np.ndarray - Phase-calibrated CSI data

docs.title: Visualization
docs.text:
from wsdp.algorithms.visualization import plot_csi_heatmap

plot_csi_heatmap(
    csi_data: np.ndarray,
    antenna_idx: int = 0,
    save_path: Optional[str] = None,
)

docs.title: CSIModel
docs.text:
CNN + Transformer architecture for CSI classification.

from wsdp.models import CSIModel

model = CSIModel(
    num_classes: int,
    input_shape: Tuple[int, int, int] = (200, 30, 3),
)

Parameters: - num_classes: int - Number of output classes - input_shape: tuple - Input shape (T, F, A)

Methods: - forward(x) - Forward pass - predict(x) - Inference

docs.title: wsdp.predict()
docs.text:
Run inference on CSI data.

from wsdp import predict
import numpy as np

# Create sample CSI data
csi = np.random.randn(5, 200, 30, 3) + 1j * np.random.randn(5, 200, 30, 3)

# Run prediction
predictions = predict(
    csi_data: np.ndarray,
    model_path: str,
    num_classes: int = 6,
)

Parameters: - csi_data: np.ndarray - CSI data (B, T, F, A) - model_path: str - Path to model checkpoint - num_classes: int - Number of classes

Returns: np.ndarray - Predictions (B, num_classes)

docs.title: wsdp run
docs.text:
Run the full training pipeline.

wsdp run [OPTIONS] INPUT_PATH OUTPUT_FOLDER DATASET

Options:
  -m, --model-path PATH    Path to custom model
  --lr FLOAT              Learning rate
  -e, --epochs INT        Number of epochs
  -b, --batch-size INT    Batch size
  -c, --config PATH       Config file path

docs.title: wsdp download
docs.text:
Download datasets.

wsdp download [OPTIONS] DATASET_NAME DEST

Options:
  -e, --email TEXT        Email for auth
  -p, --password TEXT     Password for auth
  -t, --token TEXT        JWT token

docs.title: wsdp list
docs.text:
List available datasets.

wsdp list [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -V, --verbose   Show detailed metadata

docs.title: YAML Config File
docs.text:
# config.yaml
widar:
  learning_rate: 0.001
  num_epochs: 50
  batch_size: 64

gait:
  learning_rate: 0.0005
  num_epochs: 100
  batch_size: 32

docs.title: Type Hints
docs.text:
WSDP uses Python type hints throughout:

from typing import Optional, Tuple, Union
import numpy as np

def process_csi(
    data: np.ndarray,
    sampling_rate: float = 1000.0,
) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, dict]:
    ...

For more examples, see the examples/ directory.

docs.title: API Reference - Algorithms
docs.text: See Full API Reference for complete documentation.

docs.title: wavelet_denoise_csi()
docs.text:
Wavelet-based denoising for CSI data.

from wsdp.algorithms import wavelet_denoise_csi

denoised = wavelet_denoise_csi(csi_data, wavelet='db4', level=2)

docs.title: phase_calibration()
docs.text:
Phase error correction.

from wsdp.algorithms import phase_calibration

calibrated = phase_calibration(csi_data, method='linear')

docs.title: Visualization
docs.text:
from wsdp.algorithms.visualization import plot_csi_heatmap

plot_csi_heatmap(csi_data, antenna_idx=0, save_path='heatmap.png')

Create custom_model.py: 

class YourModel(nn.Module):
    def forward(self, x):
        # x: (Batch, Timestamp, Frequency, Antenna)
        return output

model = YourModel

docs.title: ElderAL-CSI
docs.text: 📥 Download: sdp8.org/Dataset

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
ElderAL-CSI is a dataset for elderly activity and location recognition using Wi-Fi CSI.

Property Value Format .csv Subcarriers varies CSI Shape (Time, 512, 3, 3) Complex ❌ Classes 6 activities Samples 2,400 Size ~500MB

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Recommended for quick start (smallest dataset)
wsdp download elderAL ./data --email you@example.com --password yourpassword
wsdp run ./data/elderAL ./output elderAL

from wsdp import pipeline
pipeline('./data/elderAL', './output', 'elderAL')

docs.title: Data Structure
docs.text:
data/elderAL/
├── action0_static_new/
│   ├── user0_position1_activity0/
│   └── ...
└── action1_walk_new/

Dataset hosted by SDP8.org - Official SDP Platform

docs.title: GaitID
docs.text: 📥 Download: sdp8.org/Dataset

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
GaitID is a Wi-Fi-based human gait recognition dataset for identity verification through walking patterns.

Property Value Format .dat (bfee) Subcarriers 30 CSI Shape (Time, 30, 1, 3) Complex ✅ Classes 11 gait patterns Samples 22,500 Size ~1GB

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
wsdp download gait ./data --email you@example.com --password yourpassword
wsdp run ./data/gait ./output gait

from wsdp import pipeline
pipeline('./data/gait', './output', 'gait')

Dataset hosted by SDP8.org - Official SDP Platform

docs.title: Datasets Overview
docs.text:
WSDP supports 5 built-in datasets for wireless sensing research, all hosted and maintained on SDP8.org - the official SDP platform.

🌐 Browse all datasets: sdp8.org | 📥 Download via CLI: wsdp download <dataset> ./data

Dataset Format Subcarriers Complex Scenarios Size Widar .dat (bfee) 30 ✅ Gesture recognition ~2GB Gait .dat (bfee) 30 ✅ Gait recognition ~1GB XRF55 .npy 30 ✅ Human activity ~3GB ElderAL .csv varies ❌ Elderly activity ~500MB ZTE .csv 512 ✅ CSI with I/Q ~4GB

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
🔑 Authentication: All datasets require a free SDP8.org account.

# With email/password
wsdp download elderAL ./data --email you@example.com --password yourpassword

# With JWT token
wsdp download elderAL ./data --token YOUR_JWT_TOKEN

# From Python
from wsdp import download
download('widar', './data', email='you@example.com', password='yourpassword')

docs.title: Dataset Structure
docs.text:
Organize data as: 

data/
├── elderAL/
│   ├── action0_static_new/
│   │   ├── user0_position1_activity0/
│   │   └── ...
│   └── action1_walk_new/
└── ...

docs.title: Widar3.0
docs.text: 📥 Download: sdp8.org/Dataset

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Widar3.0 is a Wi-Fi-based hand gesture recognition dataset collected with Intel IWL5300 NICs.

Property Value Format .dat (bfee) Subcarriers 30 CSI Shape (Time, 30, 1, 3) Complex ✅ Classes 6 gestures Samples 12,000 Size ~2GB

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
wsdp download widar ./data --email you@example.com --password yourpassword
wsdp run ./data/widar ./output widar

from wsdp import pipeline
pipeline('./data/widar', './output', 'widar')

docs.title: Data Structure
docs.text:
data/widar/
├── gesture_class_1/
│   ├── sample1.dat
│   └── ...
└── gesture_class_2/

Dataset hosted by SDP8.org - Official SDP Platform

docs.title: XRF55
docs.text: 📥 Download: sdp8.org/Dataset

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
XRF55 is a radio frequency dataset for human indoor action analysis with 55 activity categories.

Property Value Format .npy Subcarriers 30 CSI Shape (270, 1000) Complex ✅ Classes 55 activities Samples 9,900 Size ~3GB

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
wsdp download xrf55 ./data --email you@example.com --password yourpassword
wsdp run ./data/xrf55 ./output xrf55

from wsdp import pipeline
pipeline('./data/xrf55', './output', 'xrf55')

Dataset hosted by SDP8.org - Official SDP Platform

docs.title: ZTE
docs.text: 📥 Download: sdp8.org

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
ZTE is a CSI dataset with I/Q components collected by ZTE Corporation.

Property Value Format .csv Subcarriers 512 CSI Shape (Time, 512, 3, 3) Complex ✅ Size ~4GB

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
wsdp download zte ./data --email you@example.com --password yourpassword
wsdp run ./data/zte ./output zte

from wsdp import pipeline
pipeline('./data/zte', './output', 'zte')

docs.title: Data Structure
docs.text:
data/zte/
├── user0_pos0_action0/
│   ├── sample1.csv
│   └── ...
└── user0_pos0_action1/

Dataset hosted by SDP8.org - Official SDP Platform

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: See CHANGELOG.md on GitHub for full version history.

docs.title: v0.2.0 (2026-03-17)
docs.text:
• Initial open-source release
  • Multi-dataset support (5 datasets)
  • CLI with hyperparameter control
  • Docker support
  • Full documentation site

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: See CONTRIBUTING.md on GitHub for full contributing guidelines.

docs.title: Quick Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/yuanhao-cui/SDP-Sensing-Data-Protocol-for-Scalable-Wireless-Sensing.git
cd SDP-Sensing-Data-Protocol-for-Scalable-Wireless-Sensing
pip install -e ".[dev]"
pytest tests/ -v

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.9 or higher
  • PyTorch 1.10 or higher
  • CUDA (optional, for GPU acceleration)

docs.title: Install from PyPI (Coming Soon)
docs.text: pip install wsdp

docs.title: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/sdp-team/wsdp.git
cd wsdp

docs.title: Install in Development Mode
docs.text: pip install -e .

docs.title: Install with Development Dependencies
docs.text:
pip install -e ".[dev]"

This will install additional dependencies for testing: - pytest - pytest-cov

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
# Check CLI
wsdp --version

# Run tests
pytest tests/ -v

docs.title: Create SDP8.org Account
docs.text:
Before downloading datasets, create a free account at SDP8.org.

Your SDP8.org credentials are used for wsdp download authentication:

# Option 1: Email/password
wsdp download elderAL ./data --email you@example.com --password yourpassword

# Option 2: JWT token (from SDP8.org dashboard)
wsdp download elderAL ./data --token YOUR_JWT_TOKEN

docs.title: Build Docker Image
docs.text: docker build -t wsdp .

docs.title: Run with Docker
docs.text:
# Run pipeline
docker run -v /data:/data -v /output:/output wsdp run /data /output widar

# Download dataset
docker run -v /data:/data wsdp download widar /data

docs.title: Import Error
docs.text:
If you encounter ModuleNotFoundError, ensure you're in the correct environment:

# If using virtual environment
source venv/bin/activate  # Linux/Mac
# or
venv\Scripts\activate  # Windows

docs.title: CUDA Issues
docs.text:
For GPU support, ensure you have the correct PyTorch version:

# Check CUDA availability
python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available())"

If CUDA is not available, WSDP will fall back to CPU mode automatically.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install wsdp

docs.title: 1. Download Data
docs.text: wsdp download elderAL ./data

docs.title: 2. Train
docs.text: wsdp run ./data/elderAL ./output elderAL --lr 0.001 --epochs 50

docs.title: 3. Check Results
docs.text:
ls ./output/
# best_model.pth, confusion_matrix.png, output.log

See CLI Usage for all options.

docs.title: CLI Usage
docs.text: WSDP provides a command-line interface for common tasks.

docs.title: wsdp run
docs.text:
Run the full training pipeline.

wsdp run INPUT_PATH OUTPUT_FOLDER DATASET [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -m, --model-path PATH    Path to custom model
  --lr FLOAT              Learning rate
  -e, --epochs INT        Number of epochs
  -b, --batch-size INT    Batch size
  -c, --config PATH       Config file path

Examples:
  wsdp run ./data/elderAL ./output elderAL
  wsdp run ./data/widar ./output widar --lr 0.001 --epochs 50

docs.title: wsdp download
docs.text:
Download datasets.

wsdp download DATASET_NAME DEST [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -e, --email TEXT        Email for authentication
  -p, --password TEXT     Password for authentication
  -t, --token TEXT        JWT token

Examples:
  wsdp download elderAL ./data
  wsdp download widar ./data --token YOUR_JWT_TOKEN

docs.title: wsdp list
docs.text:
List available datasets.

wsdp list [--verbose]

docs.title: wsdp --version
docs.text:
Show version information.

wsdp --version

See API Reference for full documentation.

docs.title: YAML Config File
docs.text:
WSDP supports configuration via YAML files:

# config.yaml
widar:
  learning_rate: 0.001
  num_epochs: 50
  batch_size: 64

gait:
  learning_rate: 0.0005
  num_epochs: 100

Usage: 

wsdp run ./data/widar ./output widar --config config.yaml

docs.title: CLI Parameters
docs.text:
All hyperparameters can be overridden via CLI:

Parameter CLI Flag Default Learning Rate --lr From model_params.json Epochs --epochs From model_params.json Batch Size --batch-size From model_params.json Config File --config None

docs.title: Python API
docs.text: WSDP provides a Python API for programmatic usage.

docs.title: pipeline()
docs.text:
Run the full training pipeline.

from wsdp import pipeline

pipeline(
    input_path='./data/elderAL',
    output_folder='./output',
    dataset='elderAL',
    learning_rate=1e-3,
    num_epochs=50,
)

docs.title: download()
docs.text:
Download datasets programmatically.

from wsdp import download

download('widar', './data/widar', token='your-jwt-token')

docs.title: predict()
docs.text:
Run inference on CSI data.

from wsdp import predict
import numpy as np

csi = np.random.randn(5, 200, 30, 3) + 1j * np.random.randn(5, 200, 30, 3)
predictions = predict(csi, 'best_checkpoint.pth', num_classes=6)

docs.title: Algorithms
docs.text:
from wsdp.algorithms import wavelet_denoise_csi, phase_calibration

denoised = wavelet_denoise_csi(csi_data)
calibrated = phase_calibration(csi_data)

See API Reference for full documentation.
